brand.name: Truffle Bear Academy
brand.long_name: Truffle Bear Academy · 12-Week Truffle Scent Training
voice: Scientific authority paired with plain language.
LIMA principle — Least Intrusive, Minimally Aversive.
The dog is a partner, not a tool — 'Artisan Explorer team' not 'working dog.'
Ethical foraging and forest stewardship are non-negotiable.
Lizzy Bear (and sometimes Winny) is the in-video demonstrator.
Grounded, calm, never hyped. Teaching a craft.
Quiet Focus beats drive — engagement, not excitement.

lessons.title: Meeting the Scent
supplies: Cotton ball / tin / foil scent holder
how_to_steps: Place 2 drops of Truffle Bear Oil on a cotton swab using forceps; tuck into the tin
Present the tin at nose height and remain a 'statue' so the dog leads
Mark with crisp 'Yes!' at the exact moment of nose contact
Deliver treat within 2 seconds
Run 3-5 short reps and end while dog still wants more
trainer_notes: Reward enthusiasm not perfection
Keep sessions short so the dog stays eager
Use real truffles when available
Let the trainer choose the marker that feels natural and consistent
lessons.success_metric: Dog performs an immediate focused head-turn toward the odor source in 3 of 4 reps without handler prompting
lessons.what_comes_next: Building independent hunt drive and learning the art of source commitment

lessons.title: Source Commitment
supplies: Scented tin with high-value treat hidden inside
Floor space to set up the find
how_to_steps: Tuck the dog's favorite high-value treat directly inside the scented tin
Place tin on the floor and step back without giving a 'find it' command
Let the odor be the cue to work
Remain a 'statue' to prevent the dog from looking to you for help
Keep sessions under 5 minutes
trainer_notes: By letting the dog self-reward you start becoming 'invisible' which is the hallmark of a great handler
The dog must lead the search
lessons.success_metric: Dog self-rewards directly at source consistently and shows independent confidence in approaching the tin
lessons.what_comes_next: Teaching the dog that not every smell in the forest is the one we want

lessons.title: Scent Discrimination
how_to_steps: one with soil
trainer_notes: one with kibble)
High-value jackpot reward
Forceps
lessons.success_metric: Set up a line of three tins: scented + soil distractor + food distractor|Allow the dog to sample distractors and make a conscious choice|Jackpot reward only on correct truffle choice|On wrong choice remain neutral and simply reset the rep
lessons.what_comes_next: Never punish a wrong choice — let the lack of reward do the teaching|Keep arrays clean using forceps|Make the truffle the dog's best idea every single time

lessons.title: The Alert Foundation
how_to_steps: Choose an alert behavior (nose-touch or sit) that fits your dog
Present a visible tin and mark + jackpot the split second the alert is offered
Deliver treats directly at the tin not from your pocket
Repeat until the behavior is offered unprompted
Gradually tuck the tin slightly out of sight
trainer_notes: The alert must be the dog's idea not a commanded trick
If the dog is struggling go back to the visible tin to keep confidence high
lessons.success_metric: Dog offers chosen alert behavior unprompted on a visible tin in 4 of 5 reps
lessons.what_comes_next: Adding fluency — teaching your dog to hold the signal even when you move around

lessons.title: The Alert Fluency & The All-Clear
how_to_steps: lower-tier for All-Clear)
trainer_notes: Have the dog offer the alert and silently count to 3 while taking a slow step back
Reward only after the count holds — building duration
Run a 'blank' search with no truffle oil present
When the dog realizes there is no scent and returns to you mark and give a lower-tier reward
lessons.success_metric: By rewarding the All-Clear you remove the stress of 'having to find something' from the dog's shoulders|The dog now has a partner who can tell you when to keep looking and when to move on
lessons.what_comes_next: Dog offers sustained indication (3+ seconds) at hidden source AND successfully clears empty area in 3 of 4 trials

lessons.title: Raising the Criteria
supplies: Container of loose soil (for buried hides)
how_to_steps: Bury the tin 2 inches deep in loose soil
Observe the dog's scratch-to-source behavior before the final alert
Stand at the threshold while the dog clears the entire room
Watch how the dog 'slams' into the scent cone from several feet away
trainer_notes: Raise only ONE variable at a time (Duration OR Distance OR Distraction)
Note the scratching and deep sniffing — these are exactly what we want in the wild Oregon forests
lessons.success_metric: Dog locates buried hides 4 of 5 times AND clears full room independently while handler stays at threshold
lessons.what_comes_next: Phase 3 begins — partnership and blind searches with you trusting the dog's nose

lessons.title: Handler Reads & Blind Searches
supplies: Multiple hides set up by a partner OR pre-set hides you don't see being placed
how_to_steps: First run a search where you know the location — feel how your body naturally leans
Then run a truly blind search where you don't know the hide
Stay back and let the dog tell the story
Watch for subtle body cues: head snaps
trainer_notes: tail flags
lessons.what_comes_next: Trusting the nose only develops through blind sessions|Avoid the Clever Hans Effect — your unconscious cues can corrupt the search|Document new behaviors you spot in the dog

lessons.title: Moving Outdoors & Wind Reading
supplies: Outdoor space (grass area or open lot)
Handful of dry grass (for wind check)
how_to_steps: Check wind direction by tossing dry grass into the air
Start the dog DOWNWIND so scent blows into the nose
Use a crosswind grid pattern to cover the search area
Follow the dog's pull into the wind until pinpointed
trainer_notes: Always start the dog working INTO the wind for best advantage
Outdoor work is a major step up — wind acts as a 'scent mixer'
lessons.success_metric: Dog adapts to outdoor scent dynamics; handler reads change of behavior in open air
lessons.what_comes_next: Working in real truffle substrates — bark

lessons.title: Natural Environments & Vapor Pressure
supplies: Wet bark mulch / leaf litter / grass substrate
how_to_steps: Bury the tin 3 inches deep under wet bark mulch
Let the dog work through surface scent pooling
Wait for the dog to 'dig the odor down' until the physical source is reached
Mark only when the dog reaches the actual tin not the pooled surface scent
trainer_notes: Truffle oil is an unstable volatile organic compound — it pools in substrate air pockets
Each substrate (wet bark vs dry leaves) has its own scent signature
This is the 'Marmite effect' of truffle scent
lessons.success_metric: Dog works through surface pooling and pinpoints the buried source within the substrate
lessons.what_comes_next: Heading into Truffle Country — Oregon forest work and site stewardship

lessons.title: Forest Work & Site Stewardship
supplies: Oregon forest site (Douglas fir grove ideal)
how_to_steps: Work under a Douglas fir using a systematic grid from trunk outward along root lines
Move slowly to avoid soil compaction
Observe the dog's exact nose placement when indicating
Minimize your own footprint on the search ground
trainer_notes: Respect the mycorrhizal networks — they enable future harvests
Soil compaction is the biggest threat to future truffle growth
Run a systematic grid not random following
lessons.success_metric: Dog runs a systematic grid pattern around the tree; handler practices leave-no-trace movement
lessons.what_comes_next: Tackling the hardest hides of the course — aged and degraded scent

lessons.title: Advanced Hides & Recovery
how_to_steps: Place hides 24 hours in advance so scent is partially dissipated
When the wind shifts and the dog loses the scent watch for self-recovery
Reward the recovery behavior (re-casting circling back) just as much as the find
trainer_notes: Persistence is the hallmark of a professional truffle dog
Working through aged and degraded hides builds 'nose stamina'
lessons.success_metric: Dog self-recovers after losing scent in shifting wind without looking to handler for help
lessons.what_comes_next: Graduation — full hunt simulation and TBCH certification submission

lessons.title: Real Truffle Scenarios (Graduation)
supplies: Probe tool (NOT a shovel — small probe only)
how_to_steps: Enter the site and let the dog search
trainer_notes: On firm alert approach slowly and crouch to observe exact nose location
lessons.success_metric: Use a probe gently — never guess at the location
lessons.what_comes_next: Retrieve the find then immediately close the hole to protect the roots
